name: Blockburger v. United States
summary: Held that where the same act violates two statutory provisions, there are two offenses only if each provision requires proof of an additional fact the other does not.
significance: Establishes the foundational test for determining whether two offenses are the 'same offense' for double jeopardy purposes: each must require proof of an element the other does not. All parties agree § 924(c)(1)(A) and § 924(j) fail this test because possessing a gun is a lesser-included element of fatally using one.
